examples.sentence: During the hunt, the dogs were trained to start a fresh hare whenever the first one was lost.
examples.translation: During the hunt, the dogs were trained to set a hare running anew whenever the first one was lost.

examples.sentence: If the hunters fail to catch the initial hare, they must start a fresh hare to keep the chase exciting.
examples.translation: If the hunters fail to catch the initial hare, they must set a new hare running to keep the chase exciting.

examples.sentence: Last weekend, the group decided to start a fresh hare after the previous one ran into dense woods.
examples.translation: Last weekend, the group decided to set a new hare running after the previous one ran into dense woods.

examples.sentence: When the first hare escaped the dogs, the huntsman signaled to start a fresh hare immediately.
examples.translation: When the first hare escaped the dogs, the huntsman signaled to set a new hare running immediately.

examples.sentence: After the failed project, Jenna decided it was best to start over and rethink the entire plan.
examples.translation: After the failed project, Jenna decided it was best to begin again from the beginning and rethink the entire plan.

examples.sentence: You can always start over; mistakes happen, but what matters is how you recover.
examples.translation: You can always begin again; mistakes happen, but what matters is how you recover.

examples.sentence: If the presentation didn’t go well, we should just start over and prepare something better for tomorrow.
examples.translation: If the presentation didn’t go well, we should just begin again and prepare something better for tomorrow.

examples.sentence: After their long argument, Mike and Sarah agreed to start over and rebuild their relationship from scratch.
examples.translation: After their long argument, Mike and Sarah agreed to begin a new phase of life and rebuild their relationship from scratch.

examples.sentence: Sometimes people need to start over in a new city to find peace and happiness again.
examples.translation: Sometimes people need to begin a new phase of life in a new city to find peace and happiness again.

examples.sentence: If you’re willing to forgive, I promise we can start over and make things right this time.
examples.translation: If you’re willing to forgive, I promise we can begin anew and make things right this time.

examples.sentence: The computer crashed, so I had to start over from the beginning and rewrite the entire report.
examples.translation: The computer crashed, so I had to begin again from the beginning and rewrite the entire report.

examples.sentence: She didn’t save her draft, and now she’s forced to start over all her work.
examples.translation: She didn’t save her draft, and now she’s forced to begin again all her work.

examples.sentence: If the experiment fails, we will start over and adjust our approach accordingly.
examples.translation: If the experiment fails, we will begin again and adjust our approach accordingly.

examples.sentence: They were told to start over because the initial design didn’t meet the client’s expectations.
examples.translation: They were told to begin again because the initial design didn’t meet the client’s expectations.

examples.sentence: Everyone agreed that Maria’s solo in the concert was the star turn of the evening.
examples.translation: Everyone agreed that Maria’s solo in the concert was the standout performance attracting the most attention.

examples.sentence: At the talent show last night, Josh definitely had the star turn with his incredible magic tricks.
examples.translation: At the talent show last night, Josh definitely had the standout performance with his incredible magic tricks.

examples.sentence: The director praised Tim for delivering the star turn that captivated the entire audience.
examples.translation: The director praised Tim for delivering the standout performance that captivated the entire audience.

examples.sentence: The new musical is a star turn for Emma Thompson, showcasing her incredible range as an actress.
examples.translation: The new musical serves as the main attraction highlighting Emma Thompson’s talents.

examples.sentence: He was cast as the star turn in the play, drawing crowds eager to see his performance.
examples.translation: He was cast as the main attraction in the play, drawing crowds eager to see his performance.

examples.sentence: They designed the gala around the star turn of the evening, the world-famous violinist.
examples.translation: They designed the gala around the main attraction of the evening, the world-famous violinist.

examples.sentence: Her dance was the star turn, filled with grace and energy that left everyone in awe.
examples.translation: Her dance was an appearance displaying exceptional skill, becoming the highlight of the performance.

examples.sentence: During the show, the comedian’s star turn had the audience laughing nonstop.
examples.translation: During the show, the comedian’s appearance displaying exceptional charm was the highlight.

examples.sentence: The guitarist’s star turn on the last song stole the spotlight from the rest of the band.
examples.translation: The guitarist’s appearance displaying exceptional skill on the last song stole the spotlight from the rest of the band.

examples.sentence: For this new strategy, we need to start with a clean sheet and forget previous assumptions.
examples.translation: For this new strategy, we need to begin from scratch without carrying over previous constraints.

examples.sentence: The architect suggested starting with a clean sheet to design a building that meets modern needs.
examples.translation: The architect suggested beginning from scratch to design a building that meets modern needs.

examples.sentence: When tackling complex problems, it’s often best to start with a clean sheet rather than patch old ideas.
examples.translation: When tackling complex problems, it’s often best to begin a project without carrying over past assumptions.

examples.sentence: She opened her notebook and started with a clean sheet, ready to jot down new ideas.
examples.translation: She opened her notebook and began by using an unused sheet of paper, ready to jot down new ideas.

examples.sentence: Before the lecture, I made sure to start with a clean sheet so I could organize my notes clearly.
examples.translation: Before the lecture, I made sure to begin writing on a blank page so I could organize my notes clearly.

examples.sentence: The artist started with a clean sheet of paper, sketching the first lines of her new painting.
examples.translation: The artist began by using an actual blank sheet of paper, sketching the first lines of her new painting.

examples.sentence: To draft the report, John started with a clean sheet to avoid confusion from previous versions.
examples.translation: To draft the report, John began by using a blank page to avoid confusion from previous versions.

examples.sentence: The engineers decided to start with a clean sheet of paper to rethink the entire system design.
examples.translation: The engineers decided to begin a task completely anew, treating the situation as if no prior work existed.

examples.sentence: To innovate, sometimes you have to start with a clean sheet of paper and forget old methods.
examples.translation: To innovate, sometimes you have to begin a task completely anew and forget old methods.

examples.sentence: They promised investors that the new product would be developed starting with a clean sheet of paper.
examples.translation: They promised investors that the new product would be developed completely anew without prior assumptions.

examples.sentence: For the drawing class, the teacher gave each student a clean sheet of paper to start with.
examples.translation: For the drawing class, the teacher gave each student an actual blank sheet of paper to begin an activity.

examples.sentence: I prefer to start with a clean sheet of paper rather than use recycled pages for my notes.
examples.translation: I prefer to begin an activity by using a blank sheet rather than recycled pages for my notes.

examples.sentence: When writing the letter, she made sure to have a clean sheet of paper ready on her desk.
examples.translation: When writing the letter, she made sure to have an actual blank sheet of paper ready on her desk.

examples.sentence: He started with a clean sheet of paper and sketched the initial blueprint without distractions.
examples.translation: He began the activity using a blank piece of paper and sketched the initial blueprint.

examples.sentence: After the conflict, the team agreed to start with a clean slate and focus on future goals.
examples.translation: After the conflict, the team agreed to begin again without reference to past errors.

examples.sentence: The company offered the employee a chance to start with a clean slate following the misunderstanding.
examples.translation: The company offered the employee a chance to make a fresh start without considering past mistakes.

examples.sentence: It's important to start with a clean slate when joining a new department to avoid biases.
examples.translation: It's important to begin anew without past commitments when joining a new department.

examples.sentence: After the scandal, the politician tried to start with a clean slate by engaging in community work.
examples.translation: After the scandal, the politician tried to be treated as if there was no prior wrongdoing.

examples.sentence: The school gave students a chance to start with a clean slate at the beginning of the year.
examples.translation: The school allowed students to be treated without regard to past problems, allowing a fresh start.

examples.sentence: If you want forgiveness, sometimes you have to prove you deserve to start with a clean slate.
examples.translation: If you want forgiveness, sometimes you have to prove you deserve to be treated without past repercussions.

examples.sentence: The new manager encouraged the team to start with a clean slate after the restructuring.
examples.translation: The new manager encouraged the team to begin again without reference to past errors.

examples.sentence: After the software glitch, the developers had to start with a clean slate and rewrite the code.
examples.translation: After the software glitch, the developers had to begin again and make a fresh start.

examples.sentence: To improve results, sometimes it helps to start with a clean slate and review all procedures.
examples.translation: To improve results, sometimes it helps to begin again without past commitments and review all procedures.

examples.sentence: She wiped the board clean to start with a clean slate for the new lesson.
examples.translation: She wiped the board clean to begin again without reference to previous work for the new lesson.

examples.sentence: The blockbuster was a star vehicle for the young actor, launching his career overnight.
examples.translation: The blockbuster was a production created primarily to showcase the young actor’s talents.

examples.sentence: This play was designed as a star vehicle for the veteran actress, highlighting her dramatic skills.
examples.translation: This play was designed as a production shaped to showcase the veteran actress’s abilities.

examples.sentence: Many believe the new TV series is a star vehicle for the singer trying to break into acting.
examples.translation: Many believe the new TV series is a production created to promote the singer’s public image.

examples.sentence: The charity event became a star vehicle for the celebrity, attracting massive media attention.
examples.translation: The charity event was deliberately designed to highlight the celebrity’s public image.

examples.sentence: Her solo concert acted as a star vehicle, boosting her popularity among fans worldwide.
examples.translation: Her solo concert served as a platform to promote her abilities and public appeal.

examples.sentence: That documentary was a star vehicle for the director’s unique vision and storytelling style.
examples.translation: That documentary was a project designed to highlight the director’s distinct abilities.

examples.sentence: Despite his talent, Alex lived as a starving artist, struggling to pay rent while pursuing his paintings.
examples.translation: Despite his talent, Alex lived as a person pursuing art while experiencing financial hardship.

examples.sentence: Many starving artists find it hard to balance creative passion with the need to make a living.
examples.translation: Many artists pursuing creative careers experience financial hardship and limited income.

examples.sentence: She embraced the starving artist lifestyle, choosing artistic integrity over commercial success.
examples.translation: She embraced the lifestyle of an artist enduring financial difficulties for the sake of her art.

examples.sentence: The novel portrayed the starving artist as a romantic figure, sacrificing everything for creativity.
examples.translation: The novel depicted the cultural stereotype of an artist enduring poverty for artistic expression.

examples.sentence: In the movie, the starving artist struggled but remained true to his vision despite hardships.
examples.translation: In the movie, the artist endured poverty and personal sacrifice for the sake of art.

examples.sentence: Many people admire the starving artist archetype, seeing it as a symbol of dedication and passion.
examples.translation: Many people admire the cultural stereotype of an artist who sacrifices for their art.

examples.sentence: After skipping lunch, Tim joked, 'I’m a real starvin’ Marvin right now!'
examples.translation: After skipping lunch, Tim humorously said he was extremely hungry.

examples.sentence: Don’t let starvin’ Marvin here wait any longer; dinner is ready.
examples.translation: Don’t let the person who is extremely hungry wait any longer; dinner is ready.

examples.sentence: Whenever Sarah misses a meal, her friends tease her by calling her starvin’ Marvin.
examples.translation: Whenever Sarah misses a meal, her friends playfully call her extremely hungry.

examples.sentence: They nicknamed their skinny roommate starvin’ Marvin as a joke about his constant appetite.
examples.translation: They playfully nicknamed their thin roommate as someone perceived as perpetually hungry.

examples.sentence: Hey, starvin’ Marvin, want a snack before we head out?
examples.translation: Hey, the person humorously called starvin’ Marvin, want a snack before we leave?

examples.sentence: Despite his slender frame, Jack’s friends affectionately call him starvin’ Marvin because he’s always eating.
examples.translation: Despite being thin, Jack is playfully nicknamed starvin’ Marvin for his constant hunger.

examples.sentence: The state of affairs in the company after the merger is one of uncertainty and transition.
examples.translation: The existing situation in the company after the merger is uncertain and in transition.

examples.sentence: Given the current state of affairs, it’s clear that change is necessary to improve productivity.
examples.translation: Given the current situation, it’s clear that change is needed to improve productivity.

examples.sentence: She explained the state of affairs in the neighborhood, highlighting both progress and challenges.
examples.translation: She described the existing circumstances in the neighborhood, noting progress and problems.

examples.sentence: The state of affairs with the city’s budget is quite alarming and needs immediate attention.
examples.translation: The situation regarding the city’s budget is problematic and requires urgent care.

examples.sentence: He sarcastically commented on the state of affairs at the office after the management reshuffle.
examples.translation: He ironically remarked on the problematic situation at the office after management changes.

examples.sentence: Considering the state of affairs, it’s no wonder employees are feeling demotivated.
examples.translation: Given the unsatisfactory conditions, it’s understandable that employees feel demotivated.

examples.sentence: The poll results showed a statistical tie between the two leading candidates within the margin of error.
examples.translation: The poll results indicated indistinguishable values between candidates given the uncertainty.

examples.sentence: Because of the statistical tie, the analysts couldn’t declare a clear winner in the race.
examples.translation: Due to indistinguishable measurements, analysts couldn’t determine a definitive winner.

examples.sentence: The experiment’s data revealed a statistical tie between the two treatments, making it hard to choose one.
examples.translation: The data showed no statistically significant difference between the two treatments.

examples.sentence: Even after re-evaluation, the results remained a statistical tie, so further testing was recommended.
examples.translation: After re-evaluation, the measurements were indistinguishable, requiring more tests.

examples.sentence: They managed to stave off bankruptcy by securing emergency funding just in time.
examples.translation: They delayed bankruptcy by obtaining emergency funding temporarily.

examples.sentence: Can we stave off the deadline extension until next week to finalize the report properly?
examples.translation: Can we delay the deadline extension until next week to complete the report?

examples.sentence: The medicine helped stave off the symptoms long enough for her to recover gradually.
examples.translation: The medicine prevented the symptoms temporarily, allowing gradual recovery.

examples.sentence: He tried to stave off the sadness by staying busy with work and friends.
examples.translation: He attempted to keep sadness at bay by engaging in activities.

examples.sentence: The goalkeeper’s quick reflexes helped stave off several scoring attempts during the match.
examples.translation: The goalkeeper fended off multiple attacks effectively.

examples.sentence: We used all our resources to stave off the competitor’s influence in the market.
examples.translation: We worked to fend off the competitor’s impact and maintain our position.

examples.sentence: To stave off the approaching storm, the sailors secured the ship and prepared for rough seas.
examples.translation: They delayed or prevented the storm’s effects by securing the ship temporarily.

examples.sentence: The team used defensive tactics to stave off the opponent’s advances during the crucial minutes.
examples.translation: The team delayed or averted attacks through defensive play.

examples.sentence: We placed barriers to stave off flooding after the heavy rains.
examples.translation: We prevented or delayed flooding by placing barriers.

examples.sentence: The community organized volunteers to stave off the spread of misinformation online.
examples.translation: They worked to prevent or delay misinformation from spreading.

examples.sentence: Doctors advised him to stay away from the contaminated area until it was declared safe.
examples.translation: Doctors advised him to keep a distance from the contaminated area.

examples.sentence: I try to stay away from gossip to maintain a positive work environment.
examples.translation: I avoid contact with gossip to keep the workplace positive.

examples.sentence: They stay away from the old warehouse because it’s structurally unsafe.
examples.translation: They keep a distance from the old warehouse due to safety concerns.

examples.sentence: She asked everyone to stay away from the crime scene during the investigation.
examples.translation: She requested that people avoid the crime scene during the investigation.

examples.sentence: Many fans decided to stay away from the concert after the lead singer canceled at the last minute.
examples.translation: Many fans refrained from attending the concert after the singer canceled.

examples.sentence: Employees were told to stay away from the office during the renovation period.
examples.translation: Employees were asked not to visit the office while renovations took place.

examples.sentence: She stayed away from social events to focus on her studies before exams.
examples.translation: She refrained from participating in social events to prepare for exams.

examples.sentence: They advised tourists to stay away from the festival due to security concerns.
examples.translation: They recommended tourists not attend the festival for safety reasons.

examples.sentence: After the meeting ended, John stayed behind to discuss some issues with the manager.
examples.translation: After the meeting, John remained in the room after others left to talk with the manager.

examples.sentence: The students stayed behind after class to ask the teacher extra questions.
examples.translation: The students remained in the classroom after others left to ask questions.

examples.sentence: She stayed behind at the office to finish the report while her colleagues went home.
examples.translation: She remained at the office after others had left to complete her work.

examples.sentence: They stayed behind after the event to help clean up the venue.
examples.translation: They remained at the venue after the event ended to assist with cleaning.

examples.sentence: Despite his best efforts, Mark stayed behind his classmates in math progress this semester.
examples.translation: Despite trying, Mark lagged behind others in academic development.

examples.sentence: The company stayed behind competitors in adopting new technology last year.
examples.translation: The company lagged behind other firms in implementing new technology.

examples.sentence: If you don’t keep up with the training, you risk staying behind the rest of the team.
examples.translation: Failing to keep up means you will lag behind your teammates in progress.
